rationale: R1 is feasible as written. cmd/mindspec already owns resolvePanelGateFacts, renderPanelVerify, renderPanelTally, and tallyExitAction, while internal/panel.ResolveGateFacts accepts caller-supplied GateIO. A non-bead RevParse closure can ignore the synthetic bead/ ref and resolve panel.json.target, yielding HeadSHA/MissingRef/GitErr facts for the existing decision legs without editing internal/panel. The malformed leg (5)/(5b) messages and RawMergeFence("") are only surfaced through CLI render/exit paths for these verbs, so sanitizeNonBeadDecision can rewrite Message while preserving Action and leaving internal/instruct and internal/complete untouched. Existing byte-identity pins remain viable when sanitizer use is gated to non-bead command paths. R3 also works: Panel.Gate already has json:"gate,omitempty", and Create's MarshalIndent path remains byte-identical when CreateInput.Gate is empty. R2 and R4 are straightforward against the current regex and reviewer model fallback code.
findings: R1: zero-byte diff to internal/panel, internal/instruct, and internal/complete is achievable via cmd/mindspec fact gathering and CLI-only decision-message sanitation.
R3: omitted --gate preserves panel.json bytes because the existing Panel.Gate field is omitempty and Create can leave it as the zero value.
R2/R4: no hidden feasibility obstacle found in the pinned code.
